It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: DOWN moves BOTH characters down by 3 rows. Movement model: LEFT/RIGHT moves characters horizontally (converging/diverging), UP/DOWN moves both vertically in same direction.
- Goal model: Possibly position black character to match/overlap the yellow L-shape at [45,51]-[53,59].
- Action model: Need to test SPACE and ACTION7 for special mechanics.
- Plan: First reset with UP, then try SPACE to see if there's a special action.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what SPACE did
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

before_seg = before.segmentation
after_seg = after.segmentation

print("BEFORE SPACE:")
for n in before_seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")

print("\nAFTER SPACE:")
for n in after_seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")

# Check timer
print("\nTimer comparison:")
for n in before_seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] > 50:
        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"  Before: rows [{min(p[0] for p in b)}-{max(p[0] for p in b)}]")
for n in after_seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] > 50:
        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"  After:  rows [{min(p[0] for p in b)}-{max(p[0] for p in b)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
SPACE only changed the timer bar! The timer went from rows [10-63] to [11-63], meaning it decreased by 1 pixel. A new black segment appeared at [0,63]-[10,63] (11 pixels). So SPACE just decrements the timer without moving anything. This is a wasted action.
Now I know:
- LEFT/RIGHT: move characters horizontally
- UP/DOWN: move characters vertically  
- SPACE: only decrements timer (waste)
- Need to try ACTION7
Let me also reconsider the goal. I've been spending too many actions probing. Let me think about what the goal could be:
1. The black L-shape character needs to match the yellow L-shape
2. The dark gray L-shape might be an obstacle or something to avoid
3. Maybe I need to move the black character to overlap the yellow L-shape
The yellow L-shape is at [45,51]-[53,59]. The black character is at [15,18]-[23,26].
To get there:
- Need to move DOWN: from row 15 to row 45 = 30 rows / 3 per DOWN = 10 DOWNs
- Need to move RIGHT: from col 18 to col 51 = 33 cols / 3 per RIGHT = 11 RIGHTs
But that's 21 actions just for movement, and the human solution is ~32 actions total. That seems too many.
Wait, maybe the goal is different. Let me reconsider. Maybe I need to use MOUSE to click on something, or ACTION7 does something special.
Let me try ACTION7.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: SPACE only decrements the timer - wasted action. Movement: LEFT/RIGHT horizontal, UP/DOWN vertical (3 cells per action). Characters at original positions.
Goal model: Still unclear. Need to try ACTION7 for special mechanics.
Action model: SPACE = timer only. Need ACTION7 test.
Plan: Try ACTION7, then if it's useful, figure out the goal.
